steps.title: Separate the clauses
steps.content: The sentence first says Suzuki pedagogy is rooted in several central tenets. A new sentence then begins with a participial phrase about Shinichi Suzuki and continues with "the method emphasizes," forming its own independent clause.

steps.title: Avoid comma splices
steps.content: Choice B leaves the sentence without the needed boundary. Choice C creates awkward, ungrammatical phrasing. Choice D uses only a comma before another independent clause. The remaining choice ends the first sentence and starts the modifier cleanly. The answer is A.
